prose.en: Enforce dual authorization for implementing changes to {param:cm-5.4_prm_1}.

parameters.name: cm-5.4_prm_1
parameters.label: organization-defined system components and system-level information

guidance.en: Organizations employ dual authorization to help ensure that any changes to selected system components and information cannot occur unless two qualified individuals approve and implement such changes. The two individuals possess the skills and expertise to determine if the proposed changes are correct implementations of approved changes. The individuals are also accountable for the changes. Dual authorization may also be known as two-person control. To reduce the risk of collusion, organizations consider rotating dual authorization duties to other individuals. System-level information includes operational procedures.

prose.en: dual authorization for implementing changes to {param:cm-05.04_odp.01} is enforced;
dual authorization for implementing changes to {param:cm-05.04_odp.02} is enforced.

objects.en: procedures addressing access restrictions for changes to the system
system configuration settings and associated documentation
Organizational personnel with dual authorization enforcement responsibilities for implementing system changes
organizational personnel with information security responsibilities
Organizational processes for managing access restrictions to change

guidelines.en: system components requiring dual authorization for changes are defined;
system-level information requiring dual authorization for changes is defined;
